evals.prompt: I'm starting a new browser extension project with WXT. I want to add a popup that shows when the user clicks the toolbar icon. How do I set that up? I'm using React.
evals.expected_output: Instructions for creating a WXT project with React, creating a popup entrypoint (popup.html + React app files), adding the @wxt-dev/module-react module to wxt.config.ts, and the correct file structure.

assertions.name: mentions-popup-html
assertions.value: Does the response show or mention creating an entrypoints/popup.html file or entrypoints/popup/index.html?

assertions.name: mentions-wxt-config
assertions.description: Response shows adding the module to wxt.config.ts

assertions.name: correct-project-bootstrap
assertions.description: Response shows the wxt init command or how to install wxt
assertions.value: Does the response show how to bootstrap/create a new WXT project, such as using 'wxt init' or 'npx wxt@latest init'?

evals.prompt: I'm building a WXT extension and I want to inject a floating button into every webpage the user visits. The button should be styled with my own CSS and not be affected by the page's styles. How do I do this with a content script?
evals.expected_output: A content script using createShadowRootUi with cssInjectionMode: 'ui', importing a CSS file, the correct defineContentScript with matches: ['<all_urls>'], and explanation of why Shadow Root isolates styles.

assertions.name: uses-defineContentScript
assertions.description: Response uses defineContentScript to define the content script

assertions.name: uses-shadow-root
assertions.description: Response uses createShadowRootUi for style isolation

assertions.name: has-all-urls-matches
assertions.description: Response uses <all_urls> or a broad match pattern
assertions.value: Does the response show a matches pattern like '<all_urls>' or '*://*/*' to match all pages?

assertions.name: async-main
assertions.description: Response shows main() is async (required for createShadowRootUi)
assertions.value: Does the response show that the main function should be async (since createShadowRootUi returns a Promise)?

assertions.name: uses-content-file-naming
assertions.description: Response shows the .content. naming convention for content script files
assertions.value: Does the response show the correct file naming for a content script, such as something.content.ts or content.ts?

evals.prompt: My WXT extension needs to save user preferences (dark mode toggle and a custom URL) that persist across browser restarts. I also want to watch for changes to these settings from my background script. How do I use WXT's storage API for this?
evals.expected_output: Shows storage.defineItem for typed storage items with fallback values, the 'local:' prefix, the 'storage' permission in wxt.config.ts, and storage.watch or the .watch() method on a defined item.

assertions.name: uses-defineItem
assertions.description: Response uses storage.defineItem to create typed storage items

assertions.name: uses-local-prefix
assertions.description: Response uses the 'local:' prefix for storage keys

assertions.name: mentions-storage-permission
assertions.description: Response mentions adding the storage permission to the manifest
assertions.value: Does the response mention adding 'storage' to the permissions array in wxt.config.ts or the manifest?

assertions.name: shows-watch
assertions.description: Response shows how to watch for storage changes
assertions.value: Does the response show how to watch for changes to stored values, using storage.watch() or the .watch() method?

assertions.name: shows-fallback
assertions.description: Response shows how to set default/fallback values
assertions.value: Does the response show how to define default or fallback values for storage items?
